name: admin-page
summary: Add a top-level WordPress admin menu page via a Ready event listener.
description: Scaffolds a Ready listener that hooks admin_menu and renders a top-level wp-admin page. Includes a default capability check and a render() method ready to be filled in.
problem: You need a custom admin page (settings, dashboard, status panel) and you want it registered through PHPNomad's listener pattern instead of dropping admin_menu hooks ad hoc.
appliesWhen: You need a top-level menu item in wp-admin
The page is rendered server-side (PHP) or hosts a JS app mount point
Capability-based access is sufficient (no per-user ACL)
avoidWhen: You want a submenu under an existing menu (fork the template to use add_submenu_page)
You're building a Settings API screen (consider register_setting + Settings API hooks instead)
examples: Add a Settings page under the plugin's menu
Create a Status Dashboard admin page
tradeoffs: One class plus a listener registration. Renders inline HTML — for richer UIs, mount a JS app inside render() and enqueue scripts via phpnomad/scripts-bundle.
outputs: A {name} class implementing CanHandle that adds the menu page
A getListeners() entry mapping Ready to {name}
postApply: Fill in render() with the actual page contents
Adjust the capability if the default 'manage_options' isn't right
Customize iconUrl (a dashicon slug like 'dashicons-chart-bar', or a URL)

name.aiHint: PascalCase. Suffix with Page makes the role obvious.

pageTitle.description: Browser tab title and page heading

menuTitle.description: Menu label in the wp-admin sidebar

menuSlug.aiHint: Plugin-prefixed to avoid collisions with other menus.

capability.description: WordPress capability required to see and access the page
capability.aiHint: Use a built-in capability when possible: manage_options for site admins, edit_posts for editors.

iconUrl.aiHint: Use a dashicons-* slug for built-in icons, or 'none' for no icon.
